prompt:
You are extracting atomic facts from a topic document.

The topic document below contains all source text for one specific topic, assembled with provenance headings. Your output must pass all Five Tests.

**Test 1 — Stand-Alone**: A reader with no other facts must fully understand the policy. No implied "who does this apply to?", "when does it apply?", "except what?". Include the subject explicitly in every fact.

**Test 2 — One Topic**: A single noun phrase names the subject. If "and" is needed to name the subject, split into two facts.

**Test 3 — Reconstruction**: Concatenating all your facts must be informationally equivalent to the topic document: same obligations, permissions, exceptions, numerical values. No information omitted, none added.

**Test 4 — Canonical Form**: Active voice. No document-navigation language ("as mentioned in", "refer to", "see above", "this section"). No redundant preamble ("it is important to note that", "this policy states", "please be aware").

**Test 5 — Template-Slot Ready**: No audience-specific framing baked in ("as a parent, you should…", "staff are reminded that…"). No channel-specific framing ("as you read this handbook…").

Instructions:
- This is the only pass that writes content — rewrite source text into canonical form
- Eliminate redundancy across provenance spans (same obligation stated in multiple spans → one fact)
- Split only at true topic boundaries (when the subject noun phrase changes)
- `title`: short noun phrase naming the subject of this fact
- `kind`: assign ONLY for clearly non-policy content — 'rationale', 'principle', or 'context'. Leave null for all policy facts (rules, procedures, fees, contact details, definitions)
- `topic_tags`: kebab-case tags, consistent with the topic_slug used in grouping

Audience scope: parents, staff, students
Channel scope: parent-handbook, staff-handbook, policy-documents, website

Topic document:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

Not acceptable:
* Mini-skirts, hot pants, or lycra cycling shorts shorter than mid-thigh.
* Leisure shorts, tracksuits, or sportswear (except for PE and associated activities).
* Transparent or see-through blouses, dresses, or shirts; low-cut tops; crop-tops; vest/spaghetti-strap tops.
* Clothing with tears, holes, or rips; clothes bearing offensive logos, slogans, or graphics.
* Indoor wearing of baseball caps or hats; high-heeled stiletto shoes or beach flip-flops (indoors).
* Clothes that restrict movement or can snag on equipment (loose pockets, sequins, large buttons).
* Underwear worn in a revealing manner.
raw_response: {"facts":[{"title":"Prohibited clothing items for staff","content":"Staff members are not permitted to wear mini-skirts, hot pants, or lycra cycling shorts that are shorter than mid-thigh.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff"]},{"title":"Prohibited leisure wear for staff","content":"Leisure shorts, tracksuits, or sportswear are not allowed for staff members, except when engaged in PE and associated activities.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff"]},{"title":"Prohibited revealing clothing for staff","content":"Staff members must not wear transparent or see-through blouses, dresses, or shirts, as well as low-cut tops, crop-tops, and vest or spaghetti-strap tops.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff"]},{"title":"Clothing condition restrictions for staff","content":"Staff members are not allowed to wear clothes with tears, holes, rips, or clothes featuring offensive logos, slogans, or graphics.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff"]},{"title":"Prohibited headwear and footwear indoors for staff","content":"Staff members must not wear baseball caps, hats, high-heeled stiletto shoes, or beach flip-flops indoors.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff"]},{"title":"Clothing safety requirements for staff","content":"Staff must avoid wearing clothes that restrict movement or can snag on equipment, such as those with loose pockets, sequins, or large buttons.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff"]},{"title":"Underwear visibility restriction for staff","content":"Staff members should ensure that underwear is not worn in a revealing manner.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff"]}]}

facts.title: Prohibited clothing items for staff
facts.content: Staff members are not permitted to wear mini-skirts, hot pants, or lycra cycling shorts that are shorter than mid-thigh.

facts.title: Prohibited leisure wear for staff
facts.content: Leisure shorts, tracksuits, or sportswear are not allowed for staff members, except when engaged in PE and associated activities.

facts.title: Prohibited revealing clothing for staff
facts.content: Staff members must not wear transparent or see-through blouses, dresses, or shirts, as well as low-cut tops, crop-tops, and vest or spaghetti-strap tops.

facts.title: Clothing condition restrictions for staff
facts.content: Staff members are not allowed to wear clothes with tears, holes, rips, or clothes featuring offensive logos, slogans, or graphics.

facts.title: Prohibited headwear and footwear indoors for staff
facts.content: Staff members must not wear baseball caps, hats, high-heeled stiletto shoes, or beach flip-flops indoors.

facts.title: Clothing safety requirements for staff
facts.content: Staff must avoid wearing clothes that restrict movement or can snag on equipment, such as those with loose pockets, sequins, or large buttons.

facts.title: Underwear visibility restriction for staff
facts.content: Staff members should ensure that underwear is not worn in a revealing manner.
